test_results.description: Parent process killed via Stop-Process (PowerShell). Child Python processes survived (Windows does not cascade-kill by default without /T flag). Each child had its own copy of the module-level _ledger_prior_hash global. Orphan wrote with stale hash -> chain breaks.
test_results.reason: threading.Lock is process-local. It prevents concurrent threads WITHIN one process from racing. It has no effect on two SEPARATE Python processes writing to the same file.
test_results.windows_behavior: Python open('file', 'a') on Windows does not acquire an OS-level exclusive lock. Two processes can append simultaneously. The K487 bug was specifically the stale _ledger_prior_hash global per-process, not interleaved byte-level writes (those are rare with small JSONL lines).
test_results.kill_command: taskkill /F /T /PID <pid>  (kills parent AND all children; /T flag is critical)

primary_fix: taskkill /F /T /PID — kills entire process tree on Windows
